compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- The Eternal Cylinder
- 865680

## System Information
Output of `inxi -SMG`
```
System:
  Host: asus-desk-1 Kernel: 5.15.71-1-MANJARO arch: x86_64 bits: 64
    Desktop: Qtile v: 0.22.1 Distro: Manjaro Linux
Machine:
  Type: Desktop Mobo: ASUSTeK model: TUF GAMING X570-PLUS (WI-FI) v: Rev X.0x
    serial: <superuser required> UEFI-[Legacy]: American Megatrends v: 0807
    date: 07/08/2019
Graphics:
  Device-1: NVIDIA TU104 [GeForce RTX 2080 SUPER] driver: nvidia v: 515.76
  Display: x11 server: X.Org v: 21.1.4 driver: X: loaded: nvidia
    gpu: nvidia resolution: 1: 2560x1440~144Hz 2: 2560x1440~144Hz
  OpenGL: renderer: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 SUPER/PCIe/SSE2 v: 4.6.0 NVIDIA
    515.76
```
My steam sysinfo can be found [here](https://gist.github.com/ExpandingMan/d1823b3ced1e060bfdf6bc2d726362e6).

Tried proton experimental latest and GE-37.

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11379353/195991475-b1a9ca3c-2403-48a3-8ffb-92e3de722ac0.png)

It seems strange that it gets this error since this is a common dependency.  I was able to solve the problem by using a prefix with `vcredist2019` installed via bottles.  Game seems to run fine with DX12.  Perhaps it requires a later version of `vcredist` than proton will provision?  (I have no idea how protons provisioning works.)


## Reproduction
Just install and run as normal.

compatibility_report:
Farlight 84 crashes at Lilith splash screen after latest game update (AppID 1928420)

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/9103.
@linuxgamerlife posted on 2025-10-16T13:42:29:

[steam-1928420.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22950611/steam-1928420.log)

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Farlight 84
- Steam AppID of the game: 1928420

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 9060 XT (RADV GFX1200)
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.1.9 (RADV)
- Kernel version: 6.16.11-200.fc42.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as Gist: https://gist.github.com/linuxgamerlife/a82d769b893d55fa03f55d20fe30cb95
- Proton version: Proton 10 Beta, Proton Experimental, GE-Proton10-17, GE-Proton10-20

## I confirm:
✅ that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
✅ that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms
After the most recent Farlight 84 update, the game no longer launches under Proton.
It displays the Lilith Games splash screen, then immediately closes to the desktop.

The Proton log shows an EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION inside llhpbase.dll (launcher/protection module).
There are no Vulkan or DXVK errors; GPU initialization completes successfully.
CrashReportClient runs immediately after the game exits.

This occurs across all tested Proton versions, including ones where the game previously worked (GE-Proton10-17, Proton Experimental).

## Reproduction
1. Launch Farlight 84 on Linux using any current Proton version.
2. Wait for the Lilith Games splash screen.
3. The game closes before reaching the login screen.
symptom_summary:
用户在最新更新后，Farlight 84 在 Lilith 游戏启动画面后崩溃，无法正常启动，直接返回桌面。崩溃日志显示存在 EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION 错误。

**Tags:** Crash, Access Violation, Launch Failure

issue_title: .Net Launchers fail to start if global .NET is installed on linux host
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Tested with Baldurs Gate 3
- Steam AppID of the game: 1086940

## System Information
- GPU: Radeon 780M
- Video driver version: w/e is in linux 6.8.0-41
- Kernel version: 6.8.0-41
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://privatebin.net/?d7ef6b15785b207d=#G91y8BmZBzPnLcdsdGUdZDghqMP7Yw13CoCwgjyqbAR7
- Proton version: Experimental (9.0.202 i think it is?)
- Steam System Info : 
[log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16873645/log.txt)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Log : [steam-1086940.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16873565/steam-1086940.log)
screenshot : https://imgur.com/DntIXWq

I am a .net developer, and have DOTNET_ROOT set because it installed it in my /home directory by default, which is stupid.
However, when a game uses a .net binary, like Baldurs gate does with its launcher, and `DOTNET_ROOT` is set in the host, the DOTNET_ROOT is set in proton, which makes the game's binary crash because it might be looking for something not available in the host (in this case the .net6 desktop runtime). 

A workaround is to change the launch settings of all games that use a .net binary, but this makes no sense to me as that would be a lot of games that are possibly broken because of 1 environment variable that only makes sense to linux, not wine/proton. personally, i think a protontrick to ignore the environment variable might also do the trick, because completely ignoring it is also questionable

## Reproduction
set the `DOTNET_ROOT` environment variable and start Baldurs gate 3. even things like `--skip-launcher` will fail, as it will still run the launcher exe which is unable to load.
symptom_summary:
用户在尝试启动《博德之门3》时遇到崩溃问题，原因是设置了 `DOTNET_ROOT` 环境变量，导致游戏的 .net 启动器无法加载所需的运行时。即使使用 `--skip-launcher` 参数，游戏仍然无法正常运行。

**Tag Suggestions:** 
- Crashes
- Environment Variable Issue
- .NET Compatibility

- Steam AppID of the game: 977880

## System Information

- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.2.1/LLVM 12.0.1
- Kernel version: 5.14.2-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/FurbyonSteroid/9020dba3ee51214926f32ea1529d49e2)
- Proton version: Proton Experimental (though it doesn't matter)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
1. The game requires you to force a steam play compatibility tool (apparently there is a linux build? But that doesn't work because it can't find Eastward.exe). This is probably an oversight of the developer.

2. Game does not work in the normal mode. Window opens and closes after 2 seconds (It does start with Proton-6.16-GE-1, however the entire screen is and stays black.)
**In order for it to work you have to run it through the OpenGL version.** With that everything seems to work just fine. So playable but not with the default setting (which it probably/maybe should be able to.. unfortunately I don't know the difference between the versions because the system requirements only mention opengl)

## Reproduction
Start the game.

[steam-977880-non-opengl.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7179587/steam-977880-non-opengl.log)

issue_title: [Regression] [Proton Experimental] Mouse cursor visible in Cyberpunk 2077
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Cyberpunk 2077
- Steam AppID of the game: 1091500

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 3090
- Driver/LLVM version: Nvidia 460.73.01
- Kernel version: 5.10.0-6-amd64 (Debian Bullseye)
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/piquan/315b5a32b2a4e2b3f482b64383659f03
- Proton version: experimental-6.3-20210429

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- Elaboration: Not for this issue!  I know that CP2077 with Nvidia is considered "iffy", but this seems unrelated to the known issues.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.
Proton log attached
[steam-1091500.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6405294/steam-1091500.log)

## Symptoms

Mouse cursor visible in the middle of the screen.  Cursor is arrow-shaped, and apparently rendered post-scaling: even though my resolution in the game is set to 2880x1620 (display is 3840x2160), the cursor is quite tiny, yet with sharp definition.

The cursor stays in the center of the screen, with its hotspot right where an interaction cursor or weapon crosshair appears, while the game's UI cursor (a `[-]` look) moves normally.

## Reproduction

Happens every time I launch the game with the current version of Proton Experimental (experimental-6.3-20210429).  Tried several combos of rebooting, turning off or forcing on WM compositing (I'm using KDE's default WM, KWin), etc, but the problem was persistent.  I switched to the default Proton (5.13-6) and the problem stopped.

issue_title: Shin Megami Tensei III Nocturne HD Remaster (1413480)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Shin Megami Tensei III Nocturne HD Remaster
- Steam AppID of the game: 1413480

## System Information
- GPU: RX 5700XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.1.0
- Kernel version: 5.12.5-zen1-1-zen
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/junglerobba/a6bd50285901ea36986ea62a63e6f57f
- Proton version: Proton Experimental (experimental-6.3-20210517)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1413480.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6521952/steam-1413480.log)

## Symptoms

Game runs fine (Videos are just the test image, but they are skippable just fine), except that entering save rooms often causes the game to remain on a black screen, after which the process has to be killed. This unfortunately makes this game almost unplayable.

One thing to note is, the game seems to be locked to 30 FPS on Windows, but enabling Vsync on Proton makes it run at 60. The issue happens regardless of framerate though.

Also cloud saves do not seem to be working, though I'm not sure if this is a configuration issue with the game or actually caused by running the game via Proton.
EDIT: Cloud saves are uploaded when running the game on Windows, so that's indeed a Proton issue.

## Reproduction

Play the game up until the first save room (room with the cylinder, about 10-15 minutes in), keep reentering the room until the game remains on a black screen (generally happened to me on the 2nd try)
symptom_summary:
用户在玩《真女神转生III HD重制版》时遇到的问题包括进入保存房间后游戏常常停留在黑屏状态，导致需要强制关闭进程，几乎无法游玩。此外，云存档功能似乎无法正常工作。

Tags: Black Screen, Game Freezes, Cloud Save Issues
